codeReview.summary: The commit does most of the requested overhaul correctly: the core text tokens are flipped to white, `body`/`foreground` now resolve to `--text-primary`, hardcoded `text-[#a0a0a0]` classes were removed across the app, MaturityBadge now uses white-opacity hierarchy, and a dedicated regression test was added. But the feature does not fully satisfy its own contract yet. `ScenarioSwitcher` still renders grey placeholder text through `placeholder:text-muted`, which leaves visible non-white text on first load and violates the requirement that `--text-muted` never be used for text. The new enforcement test is also too narrow to catch that regression.

issues.description: `tokenBalance` and `depositAmount` both default to empty strings (`src/components/tokenomics/ScenarioSwitcher.tsx:85`, `:88`), so the placeholders are visible on initial render. Those two inputs still use `placeholder:text-muted` (`:206`, `:225`), and `globals.css` keeps `--text-muted: #707070` for non-text uses. That means the page still shows grey placeholder text, violating both "Every text element on the page is white" and "--text-muted is NEVER used for text, only borders."

The new regression test only scans for the literal `text-[#a0a0a0]` string (`:36-45`). It does not check `text-muted`, placeholder styling, or any computed text color behavior, so the suite can still pass while visible grey text remains in the UI (as it currently does in `ScenarioSwitcher`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared frontend validation procedure is missing a reminder that text-color sweeps must include input placeholders / pseudo-elements when a feature says all text must be white. The reviewed worker reported zero grey text after scanning common text tags, but the remaining bug is in `input::placeholder` styling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `frontend-experience-worker` step 22 in `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md` requires browser validation but does not call out placeholder/pseudo-element text. The handoff at `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-23T22-20-35-446Z__readability-white-text-everywhere-and-token-overhaul__3319043f-c4fe-43f6-82d1-9657186e69c7.json` says the browser audit queried only `p, span, a, li, td, th, h1-h6, label, summary` for grey colors, while `src/components/tokenomics/ScenarioSwitcher.tsx:206` and `:225` still use `placeholder:text-muted`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library is internally inconsistent about whether `--text-muted` is allowed for text. Some docs now say it is border-only, while other reference material still describes it as a muted text token. That contradiction can mislead future workers during text-color audits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/design-system.md:38` says `--text-muted` is "Non-interactive dividers/borders ONLY — never for text", but `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/tradebetter-exact-design-reference.md:807` still comments `--text-muted` as `deemphasized`, and `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/tradebetter-comparison-evidence.md:43` and `:59` still describe it as tradebetter muted text.

summary: Fail. The feature is close, but `ScenarioSwitcher` still renders grey placeholder text via `placeholder:text-muted`, so the site does not yet meet the all-white-text contract. The added enforcement test is narrower than the requirement, and shared-state docs still send mixed signals about `--text-muted`.
